_note: Hand-written MOCK catalog-judge output that MISSES the BOTH case: it treats the high similarity as a true duplicate and collapses the two distinct engine-specific rules into a single KEEP winner, dropping the other. The verdict-matcher MUST score this as a calibration FAIL. Demonstrates the matcher catches a judge that over-trusts the dedup similarity score over the merits.
rationale: Similarity was 0.91 so I treated them as duplicates and kept the Postgres one. (Wrong: the MySQL rule is not redundant — it encodes the opposite DDL transactionality; dropping it loses real coverage. Over-trusting the similarity score is the failure this case guards against.)
